title: Bilbao
subtitle: trip map
blurb: Four slow nights in the Basque Country — pintxos, txakoli and the Guggenheim.

base.name: Hotel Hesperia Bilbao
base.note: Campo de Volantín, right bank by the Zubizuri footbridge. Guggenheim's a short riverside walk NW; hop a bridge south for the Casco Viejo.

flights.note: North Terminal. One hold bag between us.

stays.name: Hotel Hesperia Bilbao
stays.note: Right bank by the Zubizuri footbridge. Ask for a river-side room.

catalog.name: Gure Toki
catalog.note: Creative gourmet pintxos and a properly good wine list, on Plaza Nueva. Worth the elbows. Closed Wednesdays.

catalog.name: Víctor Montes
catalog.note: Grand old dame of the square: foie with caramelised apple, Cantabrian anchovies, the classics done right.

catalog.name: Gatz
catalog.note: Locals' favourite on C/ Santa María. Best bacalao al pil-pil in the old town, and cheap with it.

catalog.name: La Viña del Ensanche
catalog.note: 1927 institution, Michelin-listed. Joselito jamón and Iberico done exceptionally. Closed Mon & Sun eve.

catalog.name: Los Fueros
catalog.note: Michelin-listed but unfussy; the grilled squid is the standout. Closed Tuesdays.

catalog.name: Gin Fizz
catalog.note: The serious mixology spot near the Guggenheim. Own-recipe cocktails, deep whisky shelf (Hibiki in stock). Small room. Closed Mon, opens 15:30.

catalog.name: La Antigua Cigarrería
catalog.note: Old-guard coctelería near Gran Vía. Heavy on atmosphere; quiet early, livens up late.

catalog.name: The Craft Tabeerna
catalog.note: Ten taps of mostly Spanish craft in the Casco Viejo. For when a real beer beats a thimble of txakoli. Closed Mon.

catalog.name: Casa Rufo
catalog.note: 75-year-old classic; the txuleta is the reason to come. Also a deli/wine shop. Closed Sun.

catalog.name: Asador Guetaria
catalog.note: Old-school asador — whole chuletón over coals, proper white tablecloths. Closed Mon.

catalog.name: Mercado de la Ribera
catalog.note: Art-deco riverside market, pintxos gallery upstairs. Best in the morning when the stalls are live.

catalog.name: Azkuna Zentroa
catalog.note: Starck-renovated culture house: 43 mad pillars, a glass-bottomed pool overhead. Free, quiet, ideal rainy-day bolt-hole.

catalog.name: Guggenheim
catalog.note: Yes, the tourist thing — but the titanium hull first thing on a weekday is worth it. Closed Mon.

catalog.name: Bizkaia Bridge
catalog.note: World's first transporter bridge, 1893. Ride the gondola across, lift up to the top walkway. Metro to Areeta/Portugalete.

catalog.name: Puerto Viejo, Algorta
catalog.note: Old fishing quarter up the hillside; seafood, txakoli and the sunset over the Abra. Metro to Algorta, walk down.

catalog.name: San Juan de Gaztelugatxe
catalog.note: Dragonstone. Stunning, but a demanding ~240-step hike and busy — needs a car or tour, and reserve ahead.

catalog.name: Mundaka
catalog.note: Sleepy surf town on the Urdaibai estuary; world-class left-hand wave, good seafood, proper wind-down.

catalog.name: Txomin Etxaniz (Getaria)
catalog.note: Cliffside txakoli bodega — Atlantic terroir, sea views. ~1hr east though: a full day trip, pairs with San Sebastián.

neighbourhoods.name: Indautxu / Ensanche
neighbourhoods.note: Where Bilbainos actually eat — no reservations, just follow the busiest bar. Poteo along Las Pozas, Ledesma & García Rivero; cocktail bars come evening (25–40 crowd, smart-casual). Buzz without the coach traffic. Your best bet for nights out.

neighbourhoods.name: Bilbao la Vieja (“Bilbi”)
neighbourhoods.note: Ex-mining quarter the artists took over — edgier, less polished, creative kitchens. After dark it's the alternative zone: underground and warehouse electronic. An afternoon-into-dinner wander when you want character over gloss.

neighbourhoods.name: Deusto
neighbourhoods.note: Student side across the water — cheapest pintxos in the city, calm and green, riverside promenade for a morning walk. Laid-back kalimotxo energy; a cheap, easy evening. Your hotel sits on this same bank, just to the east.

neighbourhoods.name: Casco Viejo
neighbourhoods.note: Approach with fortitude. Real neighbourhood life under it, but weekends bring crowds spilling into the streets — Perro, Jardines, Siete Calles, 7pm till late. You're midweek: hit Gure Toki/Gatz early, then retreat over the river. Plaza Nueva is prettiest, priciest, densest. A surgical strike, not a basecamp.
